success_looks_like: I give a new directive, the pipeline asks me to verify intent (success/failure/quality_bar/examples) extracted from my brief + audit + brainstorm — not typed by me, I just confirm or correct
Builder's prompt visibly contains my original brief and the verified intent — no more telephone game
When review finds bugs, builder actually fixes them and re-review happens (not just logged as non-fatal warnings)
At completion gate, I see my acceptance scenarios as a checklist with pass/fail and evidence per item
I can say 'amend' for a small fix without full pipeline restart, 'extend' to add scope with only delta planning
DOD in project.json reads like user stories ('open game → see X') not technical checklists ('type-check passes')
failure_looks_like: The intent fields exist in the schema but nobody reads them — ceremony that agents skip
DOD examples in docs are still technical ('component renders without errors')
Review fix cycles exist on paper but are still capped at 1 like before
Amend/extend are defined in the schema but the completion gate doc doesn't explain the workflow
Builder prompt template doesn't actually include the CEO brief — it's documented but not wired
dod.quality_bar: A new directive run with these changes should feel noticeably different — builder has my words, reviewer tests my scenarios, I review once and approve instead of back-and-forth
examples: Like how a good PM writes user stories with given/when/then that the whole team can test against
Like Devin's auto-fix loop — keeps iterating until tests pass, not just once and ship

Full workflow example — BEFORE: CEO writes 'optimize mobile' → COO scope_summary loses the why → CTO DOD says 'responsive layout' → builder makes text tiny → CEO reopens. AFTER: CEO writes 'optimize mobile' → audit+brainstorm reveal patterns → system extracts intent (success: playable on phone, touch native; failure: just shrinking desktop) → CEO verifies → builder sees original brief + intent + acceptance scenarios → reviewer traces each scenario through code → completion gate shows pass/fail per scenario → CEO approves or amends specific items

output.rationale: Process-level change to pipeline architecture. Multiple valid approaches, CEO asks question without prescribing solution, lasting consequences.

output.summary: Read vision.md, lessons/orchestration.md, lessons/agent-behavior.md, pipeline docs (00-triage, 09-execute, 11-completion, failure-handling, 07b-project-brainstorm, 05-planning), schemas (directive-json, plan-schema), rules (scope-and-dod, phase-definitions, failure-handling).
Brainstorm subsumes challenge for strategic directives.

2 rounds, 8 agents. Phase 1 proposed cheaper iteration (rejected by CEO). Phase 2 reframed to first-pass quality: unanimous root cause is intent degradation — CEO words never reach builder. Two-layer solution: (1) intent propagation + user-centric DOD + review fix cycles, (2) amend/extend at completion gate. Full discussion with CEO clarifications captured in brainstorm.md.

4 projects: (1) walkthrough-polish (P0, simple, Taylor) — testing tool ships first with pipeline scenarios. (2) first-pass-quality (P0, moderate, Taylor, depends on #1) — intent propagation, user-centric DOD, review fix cycles, pipeline reorder. (3) iteration-fallback (P1, simple, Taylor) — amend/extend/redirect, schema cleanup. (4) orchestrator-split (P2, complex, Devon) — separate orchestrator from CEO session.
Brainstorm Phase 2 thoroughly examined all pipeline docs. CTO's root cause analysis (5 failure points with specific file references) serves as audit.
CEO approved 4-project plan as-is. No modifications requested.

CTO decomposed all 4 projects into tasks with user-centric DOD. walkthrough-polish: 3 tasks (scenarios, CTO trace, report template). first-pass-quality: 8 tasks (pipeline reorder, directive DOD, clarification step, scope-and-dod rewrite, brainstorm inputs, execute updates, planner bias removal, failure patterns). iteration-fallback: 3 tasks (schema fields, completion gate rewrite, failure handling). orchestrator-split: 6 tasks (architecture design, agent definition, SKILL.md dispatcher, gate protocol, resume flow, integration test).
Working on existing directive/enrich-agent-behaviors branch. No worktree needed — pipeline doc changes overlap with current branch work.

3 of 4 projects completed (walkthrough-polish, first-pass-quality, iteration-fallback). Project 4 (orchestrator-split, P2) deferred — all 6 directive DOD items are met by projects 1-3. Total: 14 tasks completed across 3 projects, 1 review fix cycle in first-pass-quality.

All 3 completed projects passed review validation. 14 tasks across walkthrough-polish, first-pass-quality, iteration-fallback — all have review phases with CTO (sarah) as reviewer, all DOD criteria met.

Digest written, lessons updated (orchestration.md + skill-design.md), stale docs detected (mostly old directives referencing broadly-modified files). No follow-ups to process (audit was skipped). Deferred project (orchestrator-split) noted in digest.
